issue_title: Support multiple route for one controller (Flask, Pyramid)
issue_body:
### At present:

Only one route (method,url) per controller is handled correctly : no documentation generation for others valids routes.

**In Bottle**, that has no consequence because you need to explicitly create one controller for each route :
```
app.route('/hello/<name>', callback=self.hello2, method='POST')
```
**In Flask**, only the route corresponding to the 'first' method is handled correctly in hapic, multiples value  in _methods_ params isn't handled correctly:

```
 app.add_url_rule('/hello/<name>', "hello2", self.hello2, methods=['POST', ])
```
so documentation for :

```
 app.add_url_rule('/hello/<name>', "hello2", self.hello2, methods=['POST','GET' ])
```

will be incomplete.

**In Pyramid**, only the first route linked to a view is handled correctly in hapic.
```
configurator.add_route('hello2', '/hello/{name}', request_method='POST') 
configurator.add_view(self.hello2, route_name='hello2', renderer='json') 
```

so documentation for : 
```
configurator.add_route('hello2', '/hello/{name}', request_method='GET') 
configurator.add_route('hello2', '/hello/{name}', request_method='POST') 
configurator.add_view(self.hello2, route_name='hello2', renderer='json') 
```
will be incomplete.


### Expected:

Be able to support multiple route for one controllers according to context, Find_route method for each context should return multiple route instead of only one.

I am upgrading a (closed-source) project to Aspen 1.0rc2. In one simplate I conditionally assign a variable `output` in the request-time Python section, and then conditionally show a message based on the truthiness of the variable. Under 1.0rc2, there is an `output` object in the default simplate context, so the value is always truthy. I know we've touched on this area before, but I forget where things stand: am I supposed to know this somehow and just be careful with a name like that?

I think the only place we ever really hit this was with `response`, because we'd do an HTTP call from inside a simplate and bind the result to `response`, which would overwrite the in-progress *Aspen* response. Should we rename this to `aspen_output` to avoid the conflict? Should we prefix other names, like `path` and `qs`?

```
if foo:
    output = 'bar'
[---]
{% if output %}{{ output }}{% endif %}
```

issue_title: Unwrap "max retries exceeded" errors
issue_labels: [{'id': 272789317, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20error%20handling', 'name': 'area: error handling', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': True}, {'id': 281347824, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/has%20pr', 'name': 'has pr', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
When there is some sort of network problem, or the server is unavailable, we get an error like:

> An unexpected error occurred: ConnectionError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='acme-v01.api.letsencrypt.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /directory (Caused by NewConnectionError('<requests.packages.urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection object at 0x7f020eee70d0>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno -2] Name or service not known',))

This is confusing because it emphasizes the "retries" part, when the important part is the "Name or service not known" part. We should handle this type of error specially and just show the actual error, not the part about exceeding retries.

issue_title: Dedupe test code
issue_labels: [{'id': 277080619, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20code%20health', 'name': 'area: code health', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 263357278, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20testing', 'name': 'area: testing', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 281347824, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/has%20pr', 'name': 'has pr', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
As pointed out in the discussion of #3972, we have some common patterns in our tests.

#### Pattern 1
`setUp()` creates a temporary directory and `tearDown()` destroys it.

#### Pattern 2
`setUp()` creates a temporary directory and creates/sets `config_dir`, `work_dir`, and/or `logs_dir` paths to be somewhere inside it. `tearDown()` destroys all directories.

Let's dedupe this code. I think the best approach is to make a class in `certbot.tests.util` called something like `TempDirTestCase` for pattern 1 that is a subclass of `unittest.TestCase` and make another class for pattern 2 that is a subclass of `TempDirTestCase`.

issue_title: Certbot opens curses sessions for informational notices, breaking automation
issue_body:
This was spotted on older clients still using the letsencrypt-auto command line. Our cron jobs: /opt/letsencrypt/letsencrypt-auto certonly --config /etc/letsencrypt/cli.ini --agree-tos --renew-by-default --expand failed with: An unexpected error occurred: Bug in pythondialog: expected an empty output from u'infobox', but got: u'Error opening terminal: unknown.\n'Please see the logfile 'certbot.log' for more details. Running interactively shows the client opening a curses dialog (where previous versions did not), although there were only an information notices displayed:

issue_title: Params with an empty cause malformed signatures
issue_body: When a param is supplied to `ToolConsumer` and it has an empty string for a value, it is used to generate the signature on the request via `generate_launch_request`, however it is not included in `generate_launch_data`. This causes problems when a server attempts to validate the signature because it won't have that additional parameter resulting in a completely different signature.

issue_title: fix handling of `CancelTransfer` messages
REST-API returns confusing 404 'invalid endpoint' error when called w/ non-checksum addresses
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Since #1506 was merged the REST-API (amongst other places) no longer accepts non-checksum (EIP55) addresses. 

Unfortunately, the error message returned if a non-checksum address is given to an endpoint is a rather confusing `{"errors": "invalid endpoint"}` with a HTTP status `404`.

We should return a proper error message informing the user about using checksummed addresses.

issue_title: Parameter for old than and newer than
issue_body:
Such a parameter can be used in `sos remove` and `%tasks` to select files, signatures, or tasks that are older or newer than specified time, but it is hard enough to differentiate between creation and modified time, older and newer, and minutes, hours, days etc. 

Note that command `find` has a bunch of such parameters and we certain do not want all of them.

```
-amin, -atime, -cmin, -ctime, -mmin, -mtime
```

Perhaps we can do something like

```
--age +1d 
--age -1d
--age +5m
```
for modified more than 1 day, less than 1day, and more than 5 minutes ago.

issue_title: task management under environments with shared home directories
issue_body:
A pretty typical academic computing environment has a number of servers with shared home directories. The `/home/user` directory is automatically mounted so that users will see the same home directory on all servers they log in. The servers themselves can have different capacities and software so that users can login to particular servers for particular purposes.

Problems, however, might arise for the management of tasks. For example,

1. What if two servers try to run the same tasks? Because of the use of lock files, one of the servers will have to wait but the reason for wait might not be clear to the user.
2. `sos purge --all` will clear all tasks, regardless of servers. 
3. A remote task can be killed locally. This is not necessarily a bad thing but could be confusing.

issue_title: TabularDataset class with header does not match fields by name
issue_body:
Currently, the TabularDataset class matches each feature in a csv/tsv file to its field by requiring the (name, Field) tuples passed to the fields kwarg to be in the same order as the columns csv/tsv file.

I think this is counterintuitive, especially if the csv/tsv file has a header. If there is a header, I think that the user should be allowed to specify the fields similar to the json format, where they pass in a dict mapping the column name to the (name, Field) tuple.

Here’s an example of what I’m thinking.

```
$ head -n 2 train.csv
"text","label"
"hello",0
```

```python
>>> pos = data.TabularDataset(
...    path='train.tsv', format='csv',
...    fields={'labels': ('labels', data.Field()),
...                'text': ('text', data.Field())}
...
```

I think they should also be allowed to select a subset of the rows using this method, similar to the API for the json format (columns not in the keys will be ignored).

This should provide a more consistent and intuitive API. If the maintainers are willing to accept this API, I would be happy to implement it and send a PR.

What is should look like:

Notice that the former one

* Doesn't align or pad the various status outputs

issue_title: Add more declarative config types
Actor creation tasks with completely infeasible resource requirements do not give warning messages.
issue_body:
First run the following code.

```python
import ray

ray.init(num_cpus=1)

@ray.remote(num_cpus=1, num_gpus=3, resources={"Custom": 2})
class Foo(object):
    pass

f = Foo.remote()
```

The actor cannot be created because the relevant resources are not available. However, because we only do spillback on the "dispatch" queue and not on the "waiting" queue, we never spillback the actor creation task, and so the warning is never triggered.

issue_title: Fix ray.wait for timeouts > 2**30
issue_body:

And make the timeout a 64-bit number.

issue_title: Can't convert '__proxy__' object to str implicitly
issue_body:
When using the Bootstrap3 inline form field template using lazily translated field labels in the current dev branch, an error is raised on:

```
{% crispy_field field 'placeholder' field.label %}
```

The error comes down to:

```
File "..../django-crispy-forms/crispy_forms/templatetags/crispy_forms_field.py" in render
  134.                     widget.attrs[attribute_name] += " " + template.Variable(attribute).resolve(context)

Exception Type: TypeError at ...
Exception Value: Can't convert '__proxy__' object to str implicitly
```

This simple `str` call resolves the issue:

```
widget.attrs[attribute_name] += " " + str(template.Variable(attribute).resolve(context))
```

Using Django 1.7c2 on Python 3.4

issue_title: Update Plugin requests policy
issue_body:
**I opened this so it won't go under.**

---

### Description

as discussed in https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1763

The **Plugin requests** Guide should be updated with some rules about plugins.

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#plugin-requests

in https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1763 were mentioned

- stolen content
- rehosted content
- NSFW sites

---

## My opinion

> stolen content / rehosted content

---

1. sites which **only focus** on stolen content should **not be added**

**Example:** _just a website with a lot of ads and some rehosted streams_

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/603
https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1207
https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1243

---

2. sites where the **User takes advantage** of the streaming site,
but it's **not** the **main focus** of the website they **could be added**.

**Example:** _a lot of people abuse youtube/twitch for sports streams, there are some other similar sites where this happens._

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/701
https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1176

---

> VOD in general which I would not add

I for one don't care for vods on smaller websites,

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1321

or if they need a lot of changes in compare to the livestream

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1733

Website with only stolen vod content

https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/issues/1110

---

And every new plugin request should have a explanation about the website which is requested,
sometimes a simple wikipedia link is enough.

If this is not given, the **plugin request should be closed** or in general if there are not details.
